page: Microsoft Azure D2_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It features 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1D cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng performance score of 1889.98 and Geekbench single-core score of 1596.0 both rank in the average tier. However, multi-core benchmarks show constraints, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1931.58 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1851.0, both placing in the bottom 10 percent. Memory performance is also limited, with uncached read and write large-block bandwidths measuring 14.67 GB/sec and 13.65 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression at 28.41 GB/sec and text processing perform at an average level, whereas database operations and Redis SET operations at 1.95 million operations per second fall into the lower performance tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2_v5 offers an entry-level dedicated resource profile suitable for workloads that do not require high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-threaded processing. Given the 1 to 4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the lack of local storage, this instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads. These include basic web serving, single-threaded application testing, lightweight background tasks, and small-scale text processing. It is less suitable for database hosting, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or memory-intensive applications due to its lower multi-core and memory bandwidth scores.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers average single-core performance, as shown by its stress-ng single-core score of 1889.98 and Geekbench single-core score of 1596.0. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the lower percentiles, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 1851.0 and uncached read bandwidth of 14.67 GB/sec. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient entry point for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and simple text processing, but is not recommended for database operations or intensive multi-threaded tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering average single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for lightweight single-threaded tasks.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose virtual machine with dedicated Intel processors, balanced memory, and average single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Delivers average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
Features hardware-assisted virtualization via Microsoft Hyper-V
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
